Title: Create a new channel with all required fields
Preconditions: User is logged in as Administrator.\nAdmin SPA is open at #!/workspace/catalogPublishing.
Steps:
1. Click the 'Add' button on the Channels blade.
2. Enter a unique channel name using Latin letters, numbers, or underscores (e.g., 'AutoTest_Channel').
3. Select one or more Languages from the multi-select dropdown.
4. Select one or more Currencies from the multi-select dropdown.
5. Select a Catalog from the catalog picker (e.g., 'Electronics').
6. Select an Evaluator type from the dropdown.
7. Verify the 'Create' button becomes enabled once all 5 required fields are filled.
8. Click 'Create'.
Expected Result:
1. New channel appears in the Channels grid.
2. Channel row displays the correct Name and Catalog.
3. Complete % defaults to 0%.
4. No errors in the console.
5. Create button remains disabled until all 5 fields (Name, Languages, Currencies, Catalog, Evaluator type) are populated.

Title: View channel details and product list
Preconditions: User is logged in as Administrator.\nAt least one channel exists with assigned catalog (e.g., 'qa/Electronics').
Steps: 1. Navigate to #!/workspace/catalogPublishing.\n2. Click on the 'qa' channel row in the grid.
Expected Result:
1. A product list blade opens for the selected channel.
2. Grid columns are visible: Pic, Name, SKU, Complete %.
3. Product count is displayed (e.g., 246 items).
4. Pagination controls are visible at the bottom.

Title: Edit an existing channel via context menu Manage
Preconditions: User is logged in as Administrator.\nA test channel exists (e.g., 'AutoTest_Channel').
Steps:
1. Navigate to #!/workspace/catalogPublishing.
2. Locate the 'AutoTest_Channel' row in the grid.
3. Click the context menu icon (vertical dots) in the second column of that row.
4. Select 'Manage' from the context menu to open channel settings blade.
5. Change the channel name to 'AutoTest_Renamed'.
6. Click 'Save'.
7. Verify the grid updates with the new name.
Expected Result:
1. Context menu opens with 'Manage' option.
2. Channel settings blade opens (NOT the product list).
3. Channel name is updated in the grid after save.
4. Save completes without errors.
5. Reopening via context menu > Manage shows the new name.

Note: Clicking the channel ROW opens the product list blade, NOT settings. Settings are accessed only via context menu > Manage.

Title: Delete an existing channel
Preconditions: User is logged in as Administrator.\nA test channel exists with no critical dependencies (e.g., 'AutoTest_Renamed').
Steps:
1. Navigate to #!/workspace/catalogPublishing.
2. Select the 'AutoTest_Renamed' channel via checkbox in the left grid column.
3. Click the 'Delete' button in the toolbar.
4. Confirm deletion in the dialog.
Expected Result:
1. Channel is removed from the grid.
2. Grid refreshes and channel no longer appears.
3. API returns 204 status.
4. No console errors.

Title: View product completeness percentage per channel
Preconditions: User is logged in as Administrator.\nChannel 'Test1/Clothing' exists with known completeness (83.4%).
Steps: 1. Navigate to #!/workspace/catalogPublishing.\n2. Observe the 'Complete %' column for 'Test1' channel.
Expected Result: 1. Complete % column displays '83.4%' for the Test1 channel.\n2. Values are formatted as percentages with one decimal place.\n3. Different channels show different completeness values.

Title: Filter products by incomplete completeness score
Preconditions: User is logged in as Administrator.\nChannel 'New/Beer' exists with completeness 71.1%.
Steps:
1. Navigate to #!/workspace/catalogPublishing.
2. Click on the 'New' channel to open product list.
3. Apply filter: completeness_new:[0 TO 99].
4. Observe the filtered results.
Expected Result: 1. Only products with completeness less than 100% are shown.\n2. Product count is reduced compared to unfiltered view.\n3. Each displayed product has Complete % between 0% and 99%.

Title: Sort channels by completeness percentage
Preconditions: User is logged in as Administrator.\nMultiple channels exist with varying completeness values.
Steps: 1. Navigate to #!/workspace/catalogPublishing.\n2. Click on the 'Complete %' column header to sort ascending.\n3. Click again to sort descending.
Expected Result: 1. Ascending sort: channels ordered from lowest to highest completeness (e.g., 0% first).\n2. Descending sort: channels ordered from highest to lowest completeness (e.g., 83.4% first).\n3. Sort indicator arrow reflects the current direction.

Title: Completeness recalculates after product edit
Preconditions: User is logged in as Administrator.\nChannel 'qa/Electronics' exists with known completeness (41.9%).\nA product in the channel has missing fields contributing to incompleteness.
Steps:
1. Navigate to #!/workspace/catalogPublishing.
2. Note the current Complete % for 'qa' channel.
3. Click on 'qa' channel to open product list.
4. Open a product with less than 100% completeness.
5. Fill in a missing required field and save the product.
6. Navigate back to the channel product list.
7. Click 'Refresh' or reload the blade.
8. Navigate back to the channels grid.
Expected Result: 1. The edited product shows an increased completeness percentage.\n2. The channel overall Complete % value is recalculated.\n3. The new percentage is higher than the previously noted value.

Title: Pagination works in channel product list
Preconditions: User is logged in as Administrator.\nChannel 'qa/Electronics' exists with 246 products across multiple pages.
Steps:
1. Navigate to #!/workspace/catalogPublishing.
2. Click on the 'qa' channel to open product list.
3. Verify page 1 is displayed with products.
4. Navigate to page 2 using pagination controls.
5. Navigate to the last page (page 5).
6. Navigate back to page 1.
Expected Result:
1. Page 1 shows the first batch of products.
2. Page 2 shows a different set of products.
3. Last page shows remaining products (count may be less than page size).
4. Navigating back to page 1 restores the original product set.
5. Total item count (246) remains consistent across pages.

Title: Search for a product within channel product list
Preconditions: User is logged in as Administrator.\nChannel with products is open (e.g., 'qa/Electronics').\nA known product name or SKU exists in the channel.
Steps:
1. Navigate to #!/workspace/catalogPublishing.
2. Click on the 'qa' channel to open product list.
3. Enter a known product name or SKU in the search field.
4. Press Enter or click the search button.
5. Clear the search field and search again.
Expected Result: 1. Search results display only products matching the query.\n2. Product count updates to reflect filtered results.\n3. Clearing the search restores the full product list with original count.

Title: Sort products by Name and SKU columns
Preconditions: User is logged in as Administrator.\nChannel product list is open with multiple products.
Steps:
1. Navigate to #!/workspace/catalogPublishing.
2. Click on a channel to open product list.
3. Click the 'Name' column header to sort ascending.
4. Click again to sort descending.
5. Click the 'SKU' column header to sort ascending.
6. Click again to sort descending.
Expected Result:
1. Name ascending: products listed A-Z.
2. Name descending: products listed Z-A.
3. SKU ascending: SKUs listed in alphanumeric ascending order.
4. SKU descending: SKUs listed in alphanumeric descending order.
5. Sort indicator reflects the active sort column and direction.

Title: Product thumbnail images display in channel grid
Preconditions: User is logged in as Administrator.\nChannel contains products with and without images assigned.
Steps: 1. Navigate to #!/workspace/catalogPublishing.\n2. Click on a channel to open product list.\n3. Observe the 'Pic' column for products.
Expected Result:
1. Products with images show a thumbnail in the Pic column.
2. Products without images show a placeholder or empty cell.
3. Thumbnails are properly sized and not distorted.
4. No broken image icons are displayed.

Title: Attempt to create channel with missing required fields
Preconditions: User is logged in as Administrator.\nAdmin SPA is open at #!/workspace/catalogPublishing.
Steps:
1. Click the 'Add' button on the Channels blade.
2. Enter only a channel name (e.g., 'NoCatalog_Test').
3. Leave Languages, Currencies, Catalog, and Evaluator type empty.
4. Observe the 'Create' button state.
5. Fill in Languages but leave others empty — observe button.
6. Fill in Currencies but leave Catalog and Evaluator empty — observe button.
7. Fill all 5 required fields and observe button.
Expected Result:
1. Create button remains disabled when any of the 5 required fields (Name, Languages, Currencies, Catalog, Evaluator type) is empty.
2. Channel is NOT created until all required fields are populated.
3. Fields with ng-invalid-required show validation styling.
4. Create button becomes enabled only after all 5 fields are filled.
5. Name field only accepts Latin letters, numbers, and underscores.

Title: Create channel with duplicate name (allowed)
Preconditions: User is logged in as Administrator.\nA channel named '1' already exists in the grid.
Steps:
1. Click the 'Add' button on the Channels blade.
2. Enter the name '1' (which already exists).
3. Select Languages, Currencies, a different Catalog, and Evaluator type.
4. Click 'Create'.
Expected Result:
1. System allows duplicate channel names — no uniqueness validation exists.
2. Both channels appear in the grid.
3. Channels are distinguishable by their Catalog column.
4. No error or warning is displayed about duplicate names.

Note: This is documented platform behavior, not a bug.

Title: Delete a channel that contains products preserves catalog
Preconditions: User is logged in as Administrator.\nA test channel exists with an assigned catalog containing products.
Steps:
1. Navigate to #!/workspace/catalogPublishing.
2. Select a test channel (with products) via checkbox.
3. Click the 'Delete' button.
4. Observe the confirmation dialog message.
5. Confirm deletion.
6. Navigate to the associated catalog to verify products still exist.
Expected Result:
1. Channel is deleted from the grid.
2. Confirmation dialog shows generic message ('Are you sure you want to delete selected catalog publishing channels?') — no specific warning about products.
3. Products in the associated catalog are NOT deleted — only the channel association is removed.
4. Navigating to the catalog confirms products still exist intact.
